question: कौन से रोग गेहूं को प्रभावित करते हैं?

question: अपने खेत में खरपतवार का प्रबंधन कैसे करें?

question: Should I protect my crop from weather?

final_output.audio: Protecting your crop from weather is important, especially if you expect heavy rains, strong winds, or extreme heat. Could you tell me which crop you are growing and the kind of weather you are worried about?

question: तना छेदक के बारे में क्या करें?

final_output.audio: तना छेदक मुख्यतः पैंजरी के अंदर लार्वा बनाकर पौधे के तने को नुकसान पहुंचाता है; शुरुआती चरण में फसल का द्रव हृदय या सफ़ेद कलई बन जाता है। अगर संक्रमण कम है तो प्रतिरोधी किस्में लाएँ, उर्वरक संतुलित रखें और प्रभावित पौधों को उखाड़ फेंकें; साथ ही फेदरह्मोन जाल और ट्रायकोग्रामा जैसी परजीवी रिहाइशें लगाएँ। यदि रोग अधिक हो तो क्लोरोफॉस २० प्रतिशत ईसी दो लिटर प्रति एकड़ या क्लोरोएन्ट्राइलिप्रोले १८.५ प्रतिशत एससी सौ पचा मिलीलीटर प्रति हेक्टेयर का छिड़काव करें, फिर भी ४७ दिनों का प्रतीक्षा अंतराल रखें। क्या आप अपनी फसल या क्षेत्र के बारे में और जानकारी देना चाहेंगे?

question: How to identify fungal diseases?

final_output.audio: Fungal diseases usually show up as unusual spots, powdery or cotton‑like growth, water‑soaked lesions, wilting or yellowing of leaves, and sometimes a foul smell on stems or roots. Look for these signs early, compare them with pictures of common diseases for your crop, and check if the symptoms appear after rain or high humidity. Would you like advice on managing a specific fungal problem you are seeing?

question: Which crops require less water?

final_output.audio: Crops that need less water include millets such as bajra and jowar, pulses like moong, arhar and pigeon pea, oilseeds such as groundnut and mustard, and drought‑tolerant vegetables like okra and amaranth. They perform well in rain‑fed or limited‑irrigation fields. Would you like advice on how to grow any of these crops?

question: How deep should I sow maize seeds?

question: When to apply top dressing fertilizer?

final_output.audio: Top dressing is best done when the crop is in its active growth stage, usually after the first quarter of the crop has emerged and before the flowering stage, so apply it about thirty to forty days after sowing for most cereals. Make sure the soil is moist before you spread the fertilizer and water it shortly after for better absorption. Would you like specific dosage recommendations for your crop?

question: एकीकृत रोग प्रबंधन क्या है?

question: What to do about whiteflies?

final_output.audio: To manage whiteflies, first place yellow sticky traps at about fifty per hectare to catch the adults, and you can even make simple traps using painted empty tins. Then spray a neem extract at half a percent concentration every seven to ten days for two or three applications, and consider adding natural predators like ladybugs or lacewings. Would you like advice on a specific crop where you’re seeing the whiteflies?

question: How to identify aphids?

final_output.audio: Aphids are tiny soft‑bodied insects, usually green or yellowish, and you’ll find them in clusters on the underside of leaves or on new shoots. They cause leaves to curl, turn pale and become sticky from the honeydew they excrete, often leading to sooty mould on the plant. Would you like advice on how to control aphids on your crop?

question: How to improve soil fertility naturally?

final_output.audio: Use farmyard manure, compost or vermicompost and mix it into the soil each season, and add green‑leaf manure or legumes as a cover crop to add organic matter and fix nitrogen. Also apply rock‑phosphate or gypsum if your soil is low in phosphorus or calcium, and practice mulching and deep ploughing to improve structure and moisture retention. Would you like tips on making vermicompost at home?

question: कवक रोगों की पहचान कैसे करें?

final_output.audio: कवक रोग आमतौर पर पत्तियों पर हल्की पीली धब्बे और बाद में भूरे धब्बे दिखाता है, और प्रभावित भाग में पानी पतला दिख सकता है। आप पत्तियों को निकट से देख कर, धब्बों के आकार, रंग और गति को पहचान सकते हैं, और अगर रोग की प्रगति तेज हो रही हो तो फसलों का नमूना स्थानीय कृषि विभाग को भेजकर प्रयोगशाला में पुष्टि करवा सकते हैं। क्या आप इस रोग के नियंत्रण उपायों के बारे में जानना चाहेंगी?

question: What organic methods can I use for disease control?

final_output.audio: Organic disease control includes using neem oil sprays, soap water mixtures, or a dust of lime and sulfur to stop fungal spread, and applying beneficial microbes like Bacillus subtilis or Trichoderma as biocontrol agents; also practice crop rotation, removal of infected plants and good field sanitation to keep diseases low. Would you like advice on a specific crop or disease?

question: How to get rid of caterpillars?

final_output.audio: You can control caterpillars by hand‑picking them early, setting pheromone traps, and releasing natural parasitoids like Trichogramma; for heavier infestations use neem seed extract spray at four percent concentration or, if needed, a licensed chemical such as indoxacarb fourteen point five percent SC mixed at one hundred sixty milliliters per acre. Would you like advice on using these methods for a specific crop?

question: What is integrated disease management?

final_output.audio: Integrated disease management means using a mix of healthy seed, field sanitation, balanced fertilisers, timely sowing, crop rotation and, when needed, approved biocides or chemicals to keep diseases low. It combines cultural, biological and chemical tools so you reduce loss while protecting the environment. Would you like advice on managing a specific disease in your crop?

question: How to get rid of locusts?

final_output.audio: To control locusts, start by monitoring their swarms and use early‑season bait sprays approved by the Central Insecticides Board, applying a fine mist over the infested fields in the early morning or late evening. Follow up with a second spray after a few days and clear any remaining egg pods by tilling the soil. Would you like advice on which specific spray is approved for use in your area?

question: What are the common pests in rice?

final_output.audio: The common rice pests include the swarming caterpillar, white‑backed plant hopper, yellow stem borer, and brown plant hopper. question: मुझे कितनी बार कीटनाशक स्प्रे करना चाहिए?

question: Which crops are profitable this year?

final_output.audio: Good news, high‑value crops like soybean, groundnut, maize and mustard are showing strong profit potential this year, especially if you use quality seeds and follow recommended fertilizer doses. question: मक्का के लिए महत्वपूर्ण सिंचाई चरण क्या है?

final_output.audio: मक्का की बुवाई के बाद सबसे महत्वपूर्ण सिंचाई चरण फूलण (फ़्लॉवरिंग) चरण है, क्योंकि इस समय पानी की कमी से दाने घट सकते हैं। दूसरा अहम चरण किटनी (कर्नेल) या कर्ब (काब) चरण है, जब पानी की मात्रा बनाए रखनी चाहिए ताकि अंकुरण और दाने का आकार अच्छा रहे। आपके खेत की मिट्टी और मौसम के अनुसार इन दो समयों में उचित मात्रा में पानी देना उत्पादन बढ़ा सकता है। क्या आप अपने बुआई के समय या जल उपलब्धता के बारे में और जानकारी देना चाहेंगे?

question: How to control aphids in wheat?

final_output.audio: To control aphids on wheat, start with field monitoring and keep beneficial insects like ladybugs and lacewings by planting border crops such as mustard or sorghum; you can also use neem seed kernel extract at five percent for a foliar spray. If the infestation is medium, a seed treatment of imidacloprid forty five grams per kilogram of seed is effective, and for a high level you may spray a registered insecticide like thiamethoxam one hundred seventy five grams per hectare, observing the pre‑harvest interval. Would you like advice on how to apply these treatments safely?

question: How much fertilizer does mustard crop need per acre?

final_output.audio: For mustard you should apply about thirty kilograms of nitrogen, twenty kilograms of phosphorus and twenty kilograms of potash per acre, split as half the nitrogen at sowing and the rest as top‑dressing later. Do you also need advice on timing or application methods?

question: What is the extended forecast?

question: How to control mites?

final_output.audio: Mites can be reduced by cleaning field debris, rotating crops and removing weeds, then using approved neem oil or a suitable acaricide such as spiromesifen at two hundred forty milliliters per two hundred litres of water per acre for moderate infestations. Would you like advice on any specific crop affected by mites?

question: What is the recommended fertilizer dose for cotton?

final_output.audio: The recommended fertilizer dose for cotton is about seventy five kilograms nitrogen, forty kilograms phosphorus and forty kilograms potassium per acre, applied in split doses with a basal application of ten tonnes of farmyard manure per hectare. Would you like advice on the timing of these applications?

question: What is the best time to sow wheat?

final_output.audio: The best time to sow wheat is usually during the first half of October, with a sowing window from early October to mid‑November depending on your region and irrigation availability. Planting in this period allows the crop to benefit from cooler weather and sufficient moisture for good germination. Would you like advice on seed rates or fertilizer recommendations for wheat?

question: What pests attack vegetables?

final_output.audio: Common vegetable pests include aphids that sap plant juices, caterpillars that chew leaves, tomato leaf miner that tunnels leaves, and bumper bugs on gourds; you can manage them with neem spray, hand picking, crop rotation and approved pesticides when needed. Would you like advice for a specific vegetable you are growing?

question: How to treat bacterial blight?

final_output.audio: To manage bacterial blight, first remove and destroy all infected plant debris and weeds that can harbour the disease. Then spray a copper based fungicide mixed with streptomycin sulphate nine percent and tetracycline one percent before flowering, and repeat a second spray about twenty days later; you can also use a foliar spray of Pseudomonas fluorescens at two percent for mild cases. Would you like advice on how to apply these sprays or which crop varieties are more resistant?

question: क्या मुझे आज या कल अपने खेत की सिंचाई करनी चाहिए?

question: How to detect pests early?

question: What are the symptoms of rice diseases?

final_output.audio: Rice diseases show different signs. Blast starts with white to gray green spots on leaves that later turn dark with reddish edges, while stem rot begins as small black lesions on the leaf sheath near water and can cause the stem to weaken and lodge. False smut produces orange then greenish black spore balls that replace individual grains. Would you like advice on how to manage any of these diseases?

question: जंग रोग को कैसे नियंत्रित करें?

final_output.audio: जंग रोग में पत्तियों पर पीले-नारंगी रंग के हल्के धब्बे दिखते हैं, समय पर फफूंदनाशी स्प्रे और प्रतिरोधी किस्में मदद करती हैं। आप फसल की लगातार जाँच करके शुरुआती लक्षण पकड़ें और अनुशंसित फफूंदनाशी दवा जैसे प्रोपिकोनाज़ोल या एज़ॉक्सिस्ट्रोबिन लगाएँ। क्या आप इस रोग के लिए उपयुक्त फसल विकल्प भी जानना चाहेंगी?

question: What is the water requirement for soybean?

final_output.audio: Soybean needs about four to five hundred millimetres of water from planting to pod formation, so give one to two irrigations during the flowering and pod stages if rain is insufficient. You can split the water as about two hundred millimetres early and the rest later when pods develop. Would you like advice on the best irrigation timing for your field?

question: What crops can I grow in summer?

final_output.audio: You can grow summer crops such as watermelon, cucumber, bitter gourd, okra, green gram, maize, sorghum, groundnut and sunflower. These crops thrive when sown in May and June and harvested before the monsoon arrives. Would you like advice on seed treatment or fertilizer for any of these crops?

question: What is the best way to store harvested crop?

final_output.audio: Store your harvested grain in clean, dry, ventilated stores and keep the moisture content low, preferably below twelve percent, to avoid mould and insect damage. Use airtight bags or polypropylene liners, and stack the store on a raised platform to keep it away from damp ground. Would you like tips on storing a specific crop like wheat or rice?

question: चावल में जीवाणु झुलसा की पहचान और इलाज कैसे करें?

final_output.audio: चावल में जीवाणु पत्ती झुलसा रोग के लक्षण पत्ती के किनारे पर पानी जैसा धब्बा, धीरे‑धीरे सूख कर पीली‑भूरी धब्बे बनते हैं और पत्ती के सिरे से नीचे तक फैलते हैं, खासकर फूटाव अवस्था में देखें। शुरुआती चरण में जल‑निकासी का ध्यान रखें और यदि रोग अधिक हो तो प्रारम्भिक चरण में १०० से १५० भाग प्रति मिलियन के करीब स्ट्रेप्टोसाइक्लिन घोल को एक किलोग्राम पानी में मिलाकर लटकते पत्तों पर १ से २ लीटर स्प्रे करें, और फिर दाने के सेट होने से पहले दोबारा स्प्रे कर सकते हैं। क्या आप इस रोग के लिए विशेष उर्वरक या किस प्रकार की फसल आश्रित प्रबंधन के बारे में जानकारी चाहते हैं?

question: Will it rain tomorrow? Should I delay my wheat sowing?

question: Should I use treated seeds?

It is especially helpful in humid regions or when sowing in fields with a history of seed‑borne problems. Would you like guidance on which seed treatments are best for your crop?

question: सूखे के दौरान पानी का प्रबंधन कैसे करें?

final_output.audio: सूखे के दौरान जल बचाव के लिए जलसंभरण तालाब बनाएं, पर्ची‑पर्ची जलनलिका से फसल को धीरे‑धीरे पानी दें और मल्चिंग से मिट्टी की नमी रखिए। क्या आप अपनी फ़सल या खेत के आकार के बारे में बताना चाहेंगे?

question: What is the growth stages of rice?

final_output.audio: Rice grows through several key stages: first the germination stage when the seed sprouts, then the tillering stage when the young plants develop side shoots, followed by the panicle initiation stage when flower buds form, next the flowering stage when the grains are pollinated, and finally the grain‑filling stage when the rice kernels mature. Would you like tips on managing any of these stages?

question: What to do about leaf spot?

final_output.audio: Remove and destroy the infected leaves or plants, keep the field clean and use a spray of Bordeaux mixture one percent or a fungicide such as mancozeb two percent when the disease appears, and provide partial shade to reduce humidity. Do you need advice for a specific crop affected by leaf spot?

question: How will rain affect my crop?

final_output.audio: Rain can boost your crop if it arrives at the right growth stage, but heavy downpour can cause waterlogging and disease. Could you tell me which crop you are growing and your location?
